 {"id":"bd-1ywr","title":"Tests: error hint rendering for common failure modes","description":"# Goal
Prevent regressions by asserting that the most important errors render with the expected hint(s).

# Suggested Test Cases
- Missing API key for provider
- Invalid JSON in settings/config
- Missing `fd` binary when using `find`
- VCR cassette missing in playback mode
- Permission denied when reading a file in `read`

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer unit tests for pure formatting/hint mapping.
- Use integration tests where behavior depends on environment variables.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests cover at least 5 representative errors.
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and do not require network.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:23:20.447232639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:23:32.503337410Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ywr","depends_on_id":"bd-1iz5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ywr","depends_on_id":"bd-2kj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ywr","depends_on_id":"bd-3am2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1zqk","title":"Docs: releasing.md (versioning + publish + release checklist)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/releasing.md` so the release process is explicit, repeatable, and does not require reading CI YAML.

# Must Include
- Version/tag policy (link to `Release: versioning + tag strategy`)
- How to generate/update `CHANGELOG.md`
- How to publish crates (if applicable) and how it relates to tags
- How to publish GitHub Releases binaries
- Pre-release checklist:
  - CI green on main
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --all-targets`
  - benchmarks (if required for this release)
- Post-release checklist:
  - verify artifacts
  - verify install instructions

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new contributor can ship a release following only this doc + CI workflows.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:14.350241916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:32.236485623Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zqk","depends_on_id":"bd-22k8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zqk","depends_on_id":"bd-2ypy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zqk","depends_on_id":"bd-dj27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zqk","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-20c","title":"Migrate core runtime to asupersync (agent/tools/session)","description":"Background:
- Provider HTTP migration (bd-1xf) is necessary but not sufficient to remove tokio.
- Core runtime still uses tokio for agent loop, channels, tool execution, and session IO.

Scope / Steps:
1) Replace tokio runtime bootstrap in main.rs with asupersync RuntimeBuilder + reactor.
2) Migrate agent loop + tool execution to Cx-based async; replace tokio channels with asupersync equivalents.
3) Ensure session IO + indexing use asupersync-friendly APIs (no tokio traits).
4) Update tests to use LabRuntime for deterministic time/cancellation.
5) Remove tokio dependency once all call sites migrated.

Acceptance:
- pi runs end-to-end without tokio in Cargo.toml.
- Cancellation + cleanup behave correctly across agent/tool/session paths.
- Tests pass under LabRuntime.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.867634353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:00.878278870Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20c","depends_on_id":"bd-1xf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20c","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20p","title":"Workstream: extension performance + benchmark evidence complete","description":"Purpose:
- Prove extension runtime performance meets budgets and compare against legacy pi.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Benchmarks for extension load/startup, tool-call overhead, and event hook latency.
- Performance budgets + thresholds documented.
- Comparison table vs legacy (same machine/CI class).

Definition of done:
- Benchmarks run via `cargo bench` and produce stable numbers.
- Performance budgets documented and met (or justified).

Dependencies:
- Requires Rust runtime and harness stubs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:20:27.159589474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:41:44.911911769Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-1fg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-1ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20p","depends_on_id":"bd-uah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2127","title":"Proptest: session JSONL roundtrip + tree invariants","description":"## Goal
Add property-based tests for session JSONL serialization/parsing and tree invariants.

## Targets
- `src/session.rs` (SessionHeader + SessionEntry serialization)
- basic tree navigation invariants (parent/child/leaf correctness)

## Invariants
- serialize→parse roundtrips preserve semantic equality (modulo generated IDs/timestamps when applicable).
- leaf_id always points to an entry that exists.
- parentId references are either `root` or an existing entry id.
- tree navigation helpers never panic on valid sessions.

## Notes
- Keep generators small to avoid combinatorial explosion.
- When randomness finds a minimal counterexample, promote it into a deterministic regression test.

## Acceptance
- proptest suite exists and is CI-safe.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:14.246528852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:14.252349317Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2127","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ni","title":"E2E: Rust extension runtime smoke suite (sample) with verbose logs","description":"Background:
- We need an end-to-end validation that sampled extensions run in Rust with real wiring.

Steps:
- Build an E2E script that loads each sampled extension from `docs/extension-sample.json` and runs its scenarios.
- Select runtime tier **generically** based on artifact/manifest (WASM host vs PiJS JS tier); no per-extension branching.
- Capture stdout/stderr, tool outputs, and event hook logs per extension.
- Store logs as artifacts for debugging (per-extension log file).

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs: extension id, scenario id, tool/command/event type.

Acceptance:
- E2E script passes for the full sample and produces logs suitable for triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:46:59.333523573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:12:28.306938455Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-34f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3rr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2p8","title":"Extension event hooks + cancellation semantics","description":"Background:
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE defines event hooks (session_before_switch/fork/compact/etc.) that can cancel actions.
- Extension runtime must enforce ordering + cancellation and emit extension_error when handlers fail.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement event hook dispatch order and payloads for session lifecycle + tool/turn events.
2) Honor cancellation responses (e.g., session_before_switch/fork/compact) and propagate to UI/status.
3) Add tests that verify cancellation prevents side effects and errors surface correctly.
4) Add conformance fixtures for cancellation edge cases.

Acceptance:
- Event hooks run in the correct order and can veto actions.
- Cancellation is observable in UI/RPC and session state is unchanged.
- Tests + fixtures cover both success and cancel paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:45.825124669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:45.846001395Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2pzp","title":"Message queue: pending-messages UI in interactive mode","description":"# Goal
Add a UI region to interactive mode that shows queued messages (steering/follow-up) while the agent is busy.

# Required Behavior
- While busy, display:
  - number of queued steering messages
  - number of queued follow-up messages
  - preview of queued text (truncate long lines)

# UX Notes
- Keep it compact; must not overwhelm the main conversation viewport.
- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can always see what is queued and what will be sent.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:07.592964192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T00:37:41.324957804Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-2skp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-3v08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qd","title":"Define per-extension scenario suite for capture","description":"Background:
- Each extension may expose tools, slash commands, and event hooks; we must test them consistently.

Steps:
- For each sampled extension, enumerate available features (tools/commands/events).
- Define minimal smoke scenarios + edge cases (inputs, expected outputs).
- Document any required configuration or secrets (mocked).

Output:
- A scenario specification mapped to each extension in the sample manifest.

Acceptance:
- Every extension has at least one scenario per supported feature category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:28.650419393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619339695Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619279633Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extension capture scenario suite spec","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qd","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qk","title":"Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity","description":"# Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature/functionality coverage of the original Pi Agent (TypeScript) with:
- Complete conformance harness validating behavioral equivalence
- Comprehensive test coverage without mocks where possible
- Full integration with sibling libraries (asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust)
- Detailed E2E integration test scripts with verbose logging

## Background
pi_agent_rust is a Rust port of Pi Agent (TypeScript CLI) by Mario Zechner. The port uses
spec-first methodology: extract behavior from legacy → implement from spec → never translate
line-by-line. Currently at ~85% feature complete with all core functionality working.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-02)
- ✅ Core Types: All message types, content blocks, usage tracking
- ✅ 7 Built-in Tools: read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls (122 conformance tests)
- ✅ Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure (all streaming + tool use)
- ✅ Agent Runtime: Full loop with tool iteration and event callbacks
- ✅ Session Management: JSONL v3 format, tree structure, branching
- ✅ Interactive TUI: Elm Architecture with bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour
- ✅ CLI: Full argument parsing, subcommands, package management
- ✅ Authentication: auth.json storage, OAuth login/logout
- ✅ Compaction: Full context compaction with LLM summarization
- ✅ RPC Mode: Complete JSON protocol implementation
- ✅ Benchmarks: Truncation, SSE parsing with performance budgets

## Key Gaps Requiring Work
1. **HTTP Migration**: Providers still use reqwest; asupersync HTTP client stubbed
2. **Extensions Runtime**: Protocol defined but no WASM loader/runtime
3. **Session Index Integration**: Implemented but not wired into CLI
4. **Themes**: Not implemented
5. **Test Coverage**: Critical gaps in provider streaming, TUI, CLI, compaction

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All providers use asupersync HTTP client (tokio removed)
- [ ] Extension runtime loads and executes WASM extensions
- [ ] Session picker uses SQLite index for fast lookups
- [ ] Theme system loads and applies themes
- [ ] 95%+ test coverage without mocks for core paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real API calls (recorded/VCR style)
- [ ] All conformance fixtures pass
- [ ] Performance targets met (startup <100ms, binary <20MB)

## Workstreams (child features)
1. HTTP Migration (HIGH priority) - Blocks tokio removal
2. Test Coverage Expansion (HIGH priority) - Quality gate
3. Session Index Integration (MEDIUM priority) - UX improvement
4. Themes (LOW priority) - Nice to have
5. Extensions Runtime (tracked separately in existing beads bd-25q)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29.025096826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":170,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cass + EXTENSIONS.md notes for port completeness:
- Extension runtime is explicitly Node/Bun-free: Tier A WASM component (default), Tier B JS compiled to QuickJS bytecode or JS→WASM, Tier C MCP process IPC.
- Connector model: pi.tool/exec/fs/http/session/ui/events hostcalls only; capability-gated, auditable, no ambient OS access.
- QuickJS event loop must drive microtasks + hostcall futures deterministically (asupersync LabRuntime-friendly).
- Protocol is versioned JSON + WIT; extension_ui_request/response + cancelable hooks are first-class and must be covered by conformance + benchmarks.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:21Z"},{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added missing Phase-9-ish beads not previously represented in open/in-progress work:
- Release engineering workstream: bd-gqtd (with versioning/changelog/CI-matrix/GH releases/docs/badges subtasks)
- Error UX workstream: bd-3am2 (hint mapping + rendering + tests)
- Rustdoc workstream: bd-14od (public API audit + doc build check)
- Troubleshooting doc: bd-3oa9 (under docs parity bd-3m7f)

Per guidance, I did not create/claim new extension-runtime work; these are non-extension polish/release/documentation gaps.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:25:25Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added additional non-extension gap-tracking beads (docs/spec drift + AgentCx + property tests):

- bd-gor5 (workstream) Docs/spec drift fix (README/PLAN/FEATURE_PARITY)
  - bd-30zd README asupersync/tokio status correction
  - bd-jw5q PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md reconcile checklist → beads
  - bd-33jg FEATURE_PARITY.md refresh statuses + correct bead refs (themes/conformance)
  - bd-24rp AGENTS.md remove stale tokio-migration statements

- bd-3i7u asupersync: define AgentCx capability wrapper (child of bd-20c)

- bd-2lr3 (workstream) Property-based + fuzz testing for core parsers (child of bd-26s)
  - bd-30hr proptest truncation invariants
  - bd-2sl9 proptest SSE parser chunk-boundary robustness
  - bd-2127 proptest session JSONL roundtrip + tree invariants

Intent: eliminate doc drift, make the remaining live plan fully represented in Beads, and add extra safety nets (property tests) beyond legacy parity.","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qrp","title":"Settings: UI toggles (quietStartup, collapseChangelog, hideThinkingBlock, cursor, padding)","description":"# Goal
Expose common UI/display settings in `/settings` and ensure they take effect.

# Settings (legacy docs/settings.md)
- `quietStartup` (hide startup header)
- `collapseChangelog` (condense changelog)
- `hideThinkingBlock`
- `showHardwareCursor`
- `doubleEscapeAction` (tree|fork)
- `editorPaddingX`
- `autocompleteMaxVisible`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggles can be changed via `/settings`.
- [ ] Changes persist.
- [ ] Interactive UI responds immediately where feasible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:56.637633490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:22.665988486Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-1cd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-2mcr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
